chapter_summary: Four friends lower a paralysed man through the roof to reach Jesus; Jesus forgives his sins then heals him. Jesus calls Levi the tax collector and eats with sinners. He teaches about fasting and new wineskins. He declares the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.
main_lesson: Jesus has power to forgive sins AND heal our bodies — and He came for everyone who knows they need Him.

memory_verse.kjv: They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Healthy people don't need a doctor — sick people do. I didn't come for people who think they're fine. I came for everyone who knows they need Me.'

parent_guide: Mark 2 is packed with controversy — every story shows Jesus pushing boundaries the religious leaders had drawn. Key moments: the healing through the roof (vv1-12) shows Jesus' authority over both sin and sickness; calling Levi (vv13-14) shows grace reaching anyone; eating with sinners (vv15-17) defines His mission. The new wine/new wineskins image (vv21-22) helps children grasp that Jesus brings something entirely new. The Sabbath passage (vv23-28) introduces the principle that God's rules are gifts to help people, not burdens.
application_for_children: Jesus sees your need and He cares. Whether you need healing, forgiveness, or a friend — Jesus came for you. You can always bring your problems to Him, just like the four friends brought their friend.

verses.kjv: And again he entered into Capernaum, after some days; and it was noised that he was in the house.
verses.little_bible: A few days later, Jesus came back to the town of Capernaum. The news spread quickly: 'Jesus is at someone's house!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus came back to town and everyone heard about it.
verses.meaning: Word about Jesus travelled fast. Whenever He was somewhere, people came — because they knew something amazing was happening.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me remember that You are always with me, wherever I am.
verses.discussion_question: If your best friend came to town, how would you tell other people?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think news about Jesus spread so quickly? What would you do if Jesus came to your neighbourhood?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today that Jesus is real and near to all who call on Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: A crowded Middle Eastern street, people pointing and whispering, a small stone house in the background with soft warm light glowing inside. Storybook style.

verses.kjv: And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive them, no, not so much as about the door: and he preached the word unto them.
verses.little_bible: So many people crowded into the house that there was no room left — not even by the door! And Jesus taught them God's Word.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The house was so full there was no room left. Jesus taught them.
verses.meaning: People were hungry to hear Jesus. He met their hunger by teaching them God's Word — that was always His first gift.
verses.prayer: God, give me a hungry heart — I want to hear Your Word the way those people did.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been in a very crowded place? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: Why did so many people want to hear Jesus teach? What do you think made Him different from other teachers?
verses.do_it_today: Sit somewhere quiet today and read one verse from the Bible, just to listen to what God says.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small stone room packed with people of all ages sitting on the floor, standing in doorways, peering through windows, listening to Jesus in the centre. Warm lamplight.

verses.kjv: And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.
verses.little_bible: Four friends arrived, carrying their friend on a mat. He couldn't walk — and they were going to get him to Jesus no matter what.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Four friends carried their sick friend on a mat to see Jesus.
verses.meaning: This is one of the most beautiful pictures of friendship in the Bible — four people who refused to give up on their friend.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me be a good friend who helps others get to You.
verses.discussion_question: What is the kindest thing a friend has ever done for you?
verses.family_discussion: These four friends didn't give up. What does that tell us about what friendship should look like?
verses.do_it_today: Think of a friend who might need help. Do one kind thing for them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Four determined friends carrying a man on a woven mat through a bustling crowd, their faces set with purpose. Warm earthy colours, storybook style.

verses.kjv: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken it up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.
verses.little_bible: The crowd was too thick — they couldn't get inside. So they climbed up to the roof, dug a hole through it, and lowered their friend right down in front of Jesus!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They couldn't get in, so they made a hole in the roof and lowered their friend down!
verses.meaning: Nothing was going to stop these friends from getting to Jesus. They found a creative, determined way — and Jesus honoured their effort.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't give up getting to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me never give up coming to You, no matter what is in the way.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever had to try really hard to do something important? What did you do?
verses.family_discussion: The friends didn't ask permission — they just found a way. Is there something in your family's life that seems like a 'closed door' where you need to find another way to trust God?
verses.do_it_today: If something is stopping you from praying today, move it out of the way and pray anyway.
verses.illustration_prompt: Four friends on a rooftop, pulling back clay tiles and rope-lowering a man on a mat through a hole, dust drifting down into the crowded room below. Sunlight streaming through the opening.

verses.kjv: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.
verses.little_bible: Jesus saw their faith — all five of them — and said to the sick man: 'Son, your sins are forgiven.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw their faith. He said: 'Your sins are forgiven!'
verses.meaning: Jesus noticed their faith, not just their effort. And before healing the man's body, He healed the deepest thing — his sin. This tells us what Jesus cares most about.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe in You. Please forgive my sins, just like You forgave the man on the mat.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus said 'your sins are forgiven' before healing the man's legs?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean that Jesus 'saw their faith'? What does faith look like from the outside?
verses.do_it_today: Tell God something you've done wrong and ask Him to forgive you — and believe that He does.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking up gently at the man being lowered on the mat, a warm, knowing smile on His face, light streaming from above. Tender storybook style.

verses.kjv: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
verses.little_bible: But some teachers of the law were sitting there, and they were thinking hard — and not in a good way.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some religious teachers were sitting there, thinking quietly.
verses.meaning: While the crowd saw a miracle of love, the scribes saw something to complain about. Their hearts were closed.
verses.memory_phrase: Guard your heart from being closed.
verses.prayer: God, don't let me have a closed heart. Keep me open to what You are doing.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been grumpy when something good happened to someone else? What did that feel like?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think the scribes couldn't just be happy for the man who was healed?
verses.do_it_today: Celebrate someone else's good news today, even if it has nothing to do with you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A group of serious men in robes sitting in the corner of a crowded room, arms crossed, frowning, while everyone else leans forward with excitement. Warm lamplight.

verses.kjv: Why doth this man thus speak blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?
verses.little_bible: They thought: 'How can this man say such a terrible thing? Only God can forgive sins!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They thought: 'Only God can forgive sins!'
verses.meaning: The scribes were right about one thing — only God can forgive sins. They just didn't realise they were face-to-face with God.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I know that You are God. Thank You for having power to forgive me.
verses.discussion_question: If someone said 'I can forgive all your mistakes forever,' how would you know if it was true?
verses.family_discussion: The scribes were right that only God forgives sins. What does it tell us about Jesus that He could forgive?
verses.do_it_today: Thank God today specifically for forgiving your sins — name the forgiveness out loud.
verses.illustration_prompt: Scribes leaning together whispering, looking alarmed, while Jesus sits calmly at the centre of the room. Contrast of tension and peace.

verses.kjv: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
verses.little_bible: Right away, Jesus knew exactly what they were thinking in their hearts. He said: 'Why are you arguing with yourselves about this?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus knew what they were thinking. He said: 'Why are you arguing inside?'
verses.meaning: Jesus can see our hearts and our hidden thoughts. Nothing is secret from Him — and that should be both a comfort and a call to honesty.
verses.prayer: Lord, You see all my thoughts. Help me think thoughts that are true and kind.
verses.discussion_question: Does it feel strange to know that God knows your thoughts? Why?
verses.family_discussion: What would change in our lives if we remembered that Jesus sees our hearts every moment?
verses.do_it_today: Before you think something unkind today, stop and ask: 'Would I be okay if Jesus heard this?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus looking directly at the scribes in the corner, His gaze calm but knowing, while the scribes look surprised that He caught them. Warm indoor light.

verses.kjv: Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked them: 'Which is harder to say —
or
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'Which is harder — to forgive sins or to say
verses.meaning: Anyone can say 'your sins are forgiven' and nobody can check it. But if someone says 'get up and walk,' the proof is right there. Jesus was about to give proof.
verses.prayer: God, help me trust that Your Word is always true, even when I can't see the proof yet.
verses.discussion_question: How do you know when something someone says is really true?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was setting up a test. Why do you think He wanted people to see proof?
verses.do_it_today: When someone tells you something true today, say 'I believe you' — practise trusting.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus with one hand open toward the paralysed man and the other toward the scribes, a questioning but confident look. The room is hushed.

verses.kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said: 'I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority right here on earth to forgive sins.' And He turned to the man on the mat—
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I have power on earth to forgive sins.' Then He turned to the sick man.
verses.meaning: Jesus called Himself 'the Son of Man' — a title from the prophet Daniel meaning the one who would rule all things. He was claiming to be that person.
verses.memory_phrase: The Son of Man forgives sins.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Son of Man with all authority. I trust You.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to have authority? Who has authority in your life?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus claimed authority to forgive sins on earth. What does that mean for how we should respond to Him?
verses.do_it_today: Write 'Jesus has authority' somewhere you'll see it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing tall in the centre of the room, speaking with authority, turning to look at the man on the mat, the crowd watching in silence.

verses.kjv: I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
verses.little_bible: Jesus commanded: 'I tell you — get up! Pick up your mat and go home!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Get up, pick up your mat, and go home!'
verses.meaning: A simple, powerful command. No ritual, no formula — just a word from Jesus and it was done. His word has power.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when You tell me to do something, help me do it right away — like this man did.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus gave you one command today, what do you think it might be?
verses.family_discussion: The man didn't argue or ask questions — he just obeyed. What does that kind of obedience look like in our lives?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something you know God wants you to do? Do it today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus pointing upward with a firm, kind expression, the man on the mat beginning to stir, the crowd leaning forward, mouths open.

verses.kjv: And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.
verses.little_bible: And right away the man jumped up, rolled up his mat, and walked out in front of everyone! They were all amazed and praised God, saying: 'We have never seen anything like this!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man got up, grabbed his mat, and walked! Everyone cheered and praised God.
verses.meaning: When Jesus acts, everyone can see it. The healing was public, undeniable, and gave glory to God — not to Jesus' own fame.
verses.memory_phrase: We have never seen anything like this!
verses.prayer: God, You do amazing things! Thank You that Your power is real and I can see it.
verses.discussion_question: What is the most amazing thing you have ever seen? Did it make you want to praise God?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd glorified God, not Jesus by name. Why do you think Mark mentions that?
verses.do_it_today: Praise God out loud today for one specific thing He has done in your life.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man walking upright through a parted crowd, carrying a rolled mat on his shoulder, face beaming, the crowd around him with hands raised, wide eyes, joyful expressions. Golden light flooding the scene.

verses.kjv: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus went out again to the lake. Crowds came to Him from every direction, and He taught them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: By the lake, crowds came to Jesus and He taught them.
verses.meaning: Even after a dramatic miracle, Jesus kept doing what He always did — teaching. His words were just as important as His miracles.
verses.prayer: Lord, I want to learn from You. Teach me something new today.
verses.discussion_question: What is one thing you want to learn more about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was never too busy or too tired to teach. What does that tell us about how He sees people?
verses.do_it_today: Read one story from the Gospels today just to learn something new about Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing beside a shimmering lake, wind in His robe, speaking to a large crowd of people sitting on the hillside. Peaceful, golden morning light.

verses.kjv: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
verses.little_bible: As Jesus walked along, He saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at his tax booth. Jesus looked at him and said: 'Follow Me.' And Levi got up and followed Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw Levi the tax man and said, 'Follow Me.' Levi got up and followed.
verses.meaning: Tax collectors were despised — they worked for the Romans and often cheated their own people. Yet Jesus chose Levi. No one is too far gone for Jesus to call.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for calling me even though I'm not perfect. I want to follow You.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt left out or like you didn't belong? How does it feel to know Jesus would choose you?
verses.family_discussion: Why would calling a tax collector have surprised the crowd? What does it say about grace?
verses.do_it_today: If you know someone who feels left out today, include them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Levi at a stone table with coins, looking up surprised as Jesus extends His hand with a smile. Other merchants looking on in shock. Warm mid-morning light.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.
verses.little_bible: Later, Jesus was having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and people known as sinners were eating there too — because they had started following Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus ate dinner with Levi and lots of people nobody liked. They all followed Jesus.
verses.meaning: Jesus sat down at the table with people society had rejected. He didn't make them clean themselves up first. He just ate with them.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for welcoming me to Your table just as I am.
verses.discussion_question: Who do you eat lunch with at school? Is there someone you could invite to sit with you?
verses.family_discussion: Sharing a meal meant deep acceptance in that culture. Why was it a big deal that Jesus ate with sinners?
verses.do_it_today: Invite someone to share a meal or snack with you today — someone you don't usually sit with.
verses.illustration_prompt: A long table full of laughing, eating people from all walks of life, with Jesus right in the middle, laughing too. Warm firelight, colourful robes, food everywhere.

verses.kjv: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?
verses.little_bible: But the Pharisees and teachers of the law saw this and said to Jesus' disciples: 'Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Pharisees asked: 'Why does Jesus eat with bad people?'
verses.meaning: The Pharisees thought holiness meant staying away from 'bad' people. Jesus thought holiness meant bringing light into dark places.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus brings light to dark places.
verses.prayer: God, help me not to look down on people. Help me to see them the way Jesus does.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever judged someone before you really knew them? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: The Pharisees thought Jesus was doing something wrong. Who was actually right — and why?
verses.do_it_today: Say something kind today to someone you might usually ignore.
verses.illustration_prompt: Pharisees in clean white robes peering disapprovingly through a doorway at the joyful dinner table inside. The contrast between outside coldness and inside warmth.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Healthy people don't need a doctor — sick people do. I didn't come for people who think they're fine. I came for everyone who knows they need Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I came for people who know they need help, not those who think they're perfect.'
verses.meaning: This is a key statement about why Jesus came. He came for people who know they are broken. Admitting you need Him is the first step.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came for those who need Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I know I need You. Thank You for coming for me.
verses.discussion_question: What do you need Jesus for most right now?
verses.family_discussion: Is it easy or hard to admit you need God? Why do we sometimes act like we don't need Him?
verses.do_it_today: Tell Jesus one thing you need His help with today — out loud or written down.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking warmly to the Pharisees outside, one hand open in an inviting gesture, while behind Him the dinner table glows with light and life.

verses.kjv: And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
verses.little_bible: Some people asked Jesus: 'John's followers fast, the Pharisees' followers fast — so why don't Your disciples fast?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People asked Jesus: 'Why don't Your followers go without food like others do?'
verses.meaning: Fasting was a sign of mourning or serious prayer. People expected Jesus' disciples to follow the usual religious habits.
verses.prayer: God, help me follow You, not just follow rules and habits that people expect.
verses.discussion_question: Has anyone ever expected you to do something just because everyone else does it?
verses.family_discussion: What religious habits does your family have? How do we make sure they are real and not just routine?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that shows your faith is real, not just a habit.
verses.illustration_prompt: People with puzzled faces approaching Jesus while He is surrounded by His happy disciples at a meal. Warm and curious scene.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered: 'You don't stop eating and celebrating at a wedding while the groom is still there, do you? While I am with them, it is a time for joy!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You don't go without eating at a wedding party!'
verses.meaning: Jesus is the bridegroom — being with Him is like being at a wedding feast. His presence means celebration, not mourning.
verses.memory_phrase: Being with Jesus is a reason to celebrate.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that having You with me is the best reason to be joyful.
verses.discussion_question: What does a wedding celebration feel like? How can following Jesus feel like that?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus compared Himself to a bridegroom. What does that tell us about how He feels about His people?
verses.do_it_today: Do something joyful today as an act of gratitude for knowing Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful wedding feast scene — dancing, music, flowers, laughter — with Jesus sitting happily among friends. Bright colours and warm light.

verses.kjv: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
verses.little_bible: Jesus added: 'But the time will come when the groom is taken away. Then it will be right for them to fast.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'One day I will be gone. Then it will be a time to mourn.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was gently hinting at His coming death. Fasting has its place — but the right time matters.
verses.memory_phrase: There is a right time for everything.
verses.prayer: God, help me know when to celebrate and when to be serious before You.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of something that is right to do at one time but not another?
verses.family_discussion: What do you think Jesus meant about the bridegroom being taken away? How does knowing that make you feel?
verses.do_it_today: Spend one quiet minute today just being grateful that you know Jesus personally.
verses.illustration_prompt: The wedding feast going on happily, but in the distance a shadow — a hint that night is coming. Contrast of warmth and coming quiet.

verses.kjv: No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'You don't sew a brand-new patch onto an old worn coat — because when it shrinks, it will tear a bigger hole.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Don't put a new patch on an old coat — it will rip worse!'
verses.meaning: You can't patch old ways of doing things with a little bit of Jesus. He brings something completely new.
verses.prayer: Jesus, don't just patch my old life — make me completely new!
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever tried to fix something with the wrong tool? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: What 'old things' do we sometimes try to mix with Jesus instead of letting Him change us completely?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to show you one old habit He wants to make completely new in you.
verses.illustration_prompt: An old robe with a bright new patch being sewn on, then the patch pulling away and tearing — shown as two side-by-side illustrations. Simple and clear.

verses.kjv: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'And you don't pour fresh new wine into old cracked wineskins. The new wine will burst them, and you'll lose everything. New wine needs new wineskins!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'New wine goes in new wineskins. The old ones will break.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was explaining that His new way of life cannot be squeezed into old religious systems. He calls us to be completely new.
verses.memory_phrase: New life needs a new heart.
verses.prayer: God, make me a new wineskin — ready to hold everything You want to pour into me.
verses.discussion_question: What does 'new' feel like? Have you ever got something brand new?
verses.family_discussion: What does being 'new' in Christ mean for your family? Is there something old that needs to go?
verses.do_it_today: Write on paper: 'I am being made new by Jesus.' Put it somewhere you can see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two leather wineskins side by side — one cracked and old with wine spilling, one new and full and sealed. Simple, clear storybook style.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.
verses.little_bible: One Sabbath day, Jesus and His disciples were walking through some grain fields. As they walked, the disciples started picking heads of grain to eat.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: One day off, Jesus' friends picked grain while they walked through a field.
verses.meaning: Picking grain to eat while walking through a field was allowed by God's law. But the Pharisees had added many extra rules about the Sabbath.
verses.memory_phrase: God's rules are meant to help, not hurt.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me understand Your rules and love them, not just follow them out of fear.
verses.discussion_question: Do you know why we have rules? What would happen if there were no rules?
verses.family_discussion: What was the Sabbath originally for? How can your family make rest a gift rather than a burden?
verses.do_it_today: Do something restful today that helps you think about God.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and disciples walking through a golden grain field on a sunny day, some of the disciples plucking stalks as they go. Peaceful, pastoral storybook scene.

verses.kjv: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
verses.little_bible: The Pharisees saw them and said: 'Look! Why are Your disciples doing what is not allowed on the Sabbath?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Pharisees said: 'Your friends are breaking the Sabbath rules!'
verses.meaning: The Pharisees had built 39 categories of forbidden work for the Sabbath. They had turned a gift from God into a trap.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't turn God's gifts into traps.
verses.prayer: God, help me receive Your gifts as gifts — not turn them into burdens.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever turned something fun into a chore by adding too many rules?
verses.family_discussion: How do religious rules sometimes get in the way of a real relationship with God?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one thing about faith that should feel joyful. How can you make it joyful again?
verses.illustration_prompt: Pharisees pointing accusingly at the disciples in the field, stern faces, official robes, while the disciples look surprised.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered: 'Haven't you ever read what King David did when he and his men were hungry?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: 'Remember what King David did when he was hungry?'
verses.meaning: Jesus pointed back to the scriptures they claimed to know so well. Real knowledge of God's Word should lead to mercy, not legalism.
verses.memory_phrase: Know God's Word, not just the rules.
verses.prayer: God, help me read Your Word to know Your heart, not just to know the rules.
verses.discussion_question: Do you know the story of King David? What kind of man was he?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think Jesus pointed to David's example here? What was the principle He was drawing out?
verses.do_it_today: Look up the story Jesus is referring to in 1 Samuel 21:1-6 and read it together as a family.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing as if telling a story, disciples and Pharisees listening, with a thought-bubble showing a scene of King David and his men.

verses.kjv: How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?
verses.little_bible: David went into God's house when Abiathar was the high priest and ate the special bread that was only supposed to be for priests — and gave some to his men too.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: David ate the special temple bread that was only for priests, and shared it with his men.
verses.meaning: God honoured David even though he technically broke a rule — because the need was real and David's heart was right. God cares about mercy, not just rules.
verses.prayer: God, give me a heart that loves mercy more than rules. Help me to be kind.
verses.discussion_question: What does mercy mean? Can you think of a time when someone showed you mercy?
verses.family_discussion: Does God care about rules? Does He care about people more? How do we hold both things together?
verses.do_it_today: Do one act of mercy for someone today — something kind you didn't have to do.
verses.illustration_prompt: David in ancient armour receiving bread from a priest in a lamplit tabernacle, sharing it with exhausted soldiers beside him. Reverent and warm.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them: 'The Sabbath day was made to be a gift for people — people were not made to serve the Sabbath!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'The day of rest was made for people — not the other way around.'
verses.meaning: This is one of Jesus' most important statements about how to read God's law. God's rules are always for our good, never to burden us.
verses.memory_phrase: God's rules are a gift for us.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Your rules are made for my good. Help me love them.
verses.discussion_question: What is one rule that you now understand is there to help you?
verses.family_discussion: How can your family approach God's commands as gifts rather than burdens?
verses.do_it_today: Rest today in a way that is truly restful — and thank God for giving you permission to rest.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus spreading His arms wide in a field of grain under a bright blue sky, smiling — as if giving a great gift.

verses.kjv: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
verses.little_bible: Jesus concluded: 'And so the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath day too.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I am Lord over the day of rest too.'
verses.meaning: Jesus has authority over all creation — including time itself. As the Lord of the Sabbath, He can define what the Sabbath truly means.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are Lord over every day of my life. I give every day to You.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean for Jesus to be 'Lord' of something?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is Lord of the Sabbath. How does that change how we approach rest and worship?
verses.do_it_today: At the end of the day, say: 'Jesus, You are Lord of today. I give it all to You.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing on a hilltop at sunrise, arms wide, light pouring over Him — Lord of the day, Lord of time. Epic and peaceful.
